acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] StalenessChecker detects when db is behind git store
- [ ] AllowStale flag available globally
- [ ] Read-only commands auto-import on stale db
- [ ] Write commands fail fast on stale db
- [ ] Clear error messages guide user to sync
- [ ] Staleness info shows commits ahead and time delta
- [ ] Auto-import is transparent (debug logging only)
- [ ] No performance regression on staleness check
title: Implement stale database handling - AllowStale mode for read-only commands

description:
**Problem**
`bd list` and `bd search` lack flexible sorting. Go beads doesn't have explicit --sort flags but Rust implementation should add this UX improvement.

**Design**
Add sorting flags to `bd list` and `bd search`:

`--sort <field>` options:
- `priority` (default): P0 → P4
- `created`: oldest → newest
- `updated`: least recently updated → most recent
- `status`: open → in_progress → blocked → closed
- `id`: lexicographic
- `title`: alphabetic

`--reverse` flag: reverses sort order

Examples:
```bash
bd list --sort updated               # least recent first
bd list --sort priority --reverse    # P4 → P0
bd list --sort created --reverse     # newest first
bd search "auth" --sort status
```

**Design Notes**
- Default sort: priority ASC (P0 first), then created DESC (newest first)
- Implement in Rust using `sort_by_key` with custom comparators
- Status sort order: open < in_progress < blocked < deferred < closed
- Sorting happens in-memory after database query (simple, flexible)
- Works with all filters (--status, --priority, --label, etc.)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] --sort flag with options: priority, created, updated, status, id, title
- [ ] --reverse flag reverses sort order
- [ ] Default: priority ASC, created DESC
- [ ] Status sort uses semantic ordering
- [ ] Works on bd list and bd search
- [ ] Combines with existing filters
- [ ] --help documents sort field options

**Files:**
- `src/cli/list.rs`
- `src/cli/search.rs`
- `src/cli/sorting.rs` (new - shared sort logic)
title: Sorting flags --sort and --reverse for bd list/search

description:
**Problem**
Several integration helper surfaces still default to ambient IPC resolution instead of fixture-bound runtime paths. In particular, `fixtures/load_gen.rs`, `fixtures/admin_status.rs`, and `fixtures/ipc_stream.rs` can still construct `IpcClient::new()` paths that fall back to the shared per-user socket under `/tmp/beads-$UID/daemon.sock`. That is a shared-state trap for new tests and makes isolation bugs too easy to reintroduce.

**Design**
Require explicit runtime-bound clients or runtime dirs for these helpers. Remove ambient constructors where possible, or fence them behind clearly named APIs that tests do not use by accident. Update fixture guidance so new tests always bind helper traffic to the owning runtime fixture.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Integration helper APIs no longer default to ambient socket resolution
- [ ] Existing helper callsites pass explicit runtime/client ownership
- [ ] Regression coverage or assertions prevent accidental fallback to shared user sockets

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/load_gen.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/admin_status.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/ipc_stream.rs`
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs`
- `AGENTS.md`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Closed on working copy `yknuovvv` / commit `7a5b3e60` in `/Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs`.

Landed:
- removed ambient integration-helper socket resolution from `admin_status.rs`, `load_gen.rs`, and `ipc_stream.rs` in favor of explicit runtime-dir or explicit client ownership
- updated helper callsites and fixture guidance so new tests do not silently fall back to the shared per-user socket
- added runtime-socket regression assertions for the fixture constructors/helpers

Verification:
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration fixtures:: -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo xtest` (`1118 passed`)
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests` (`1239 passed`)

Adversarial review found no blockers on the Batch B diff.

title: Remove ambient socket defaults from integration helper clients
